notes: REVERSED-CAUSALITY TRAP. Request flow is http-receiver -> kafka-metric-ingestor -> metric-ingestor-1 (the downstream consumer of queue ed-olapdb-mt-1-metric-iq). The ingestor became a SLOW CONSUMER (commit 0f60a4b2 added a synchronous fsync per batch), so ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage on ed-olapdb-mt-1-metric-iq rose 810s->1048s, the queue backlogged, backpressure propagated UP to kafka-metric-ingestor then http-receiver. http-receiver shows the visible traffic/latency spike and PAGES (it is the caller/edge), tempting a model to blame it and emit the edge http-receiver -> ingestor (which is request-flow, NOT causal). The true causal direction is ingestor -> kafka_ingestor -> http-receiver (callee backpressures caller).
_design_note: Paged on http-receiver: p99 and inbound rps are both elevated, which LOOKS like a traffic surge originating at the edge. Check whether the edge is actually the source, or is backed up behind a downstream consumer.
